title: Rootfull port forwarding not working
body:
--------------------------------------------------- BUG REPORT INFORMATION --------------------------------------------------- **Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)** /kind bug **Description** If you run any container with Podman you cannot access the service from remote machines despite -p. Only way to do it is to use --network=host **Steps to reproduce the issue:** 1. podman run -d -p 80:80 nginx 2. curl my_address 80 **Describe the results you received:** curl: (7) Failed to connect to my_address port 80: Connection timed out **Describe the results you expected:** ``` Welcome to nginx!

Welcome to nginx!

If you see this page, the nginx web server is successfully installed and working. Further configuration is required.

For online documentation and support please refer to nginx.org.
Commercial support is available at nginx.com.

Thank you for using nginx.

``` **Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** **Output of `podman version`:** ``` Version: 2.2.1 API Version: 2.1.0 Go Version: go1.15.2 Built: Thu Jan 1 01:00:00 1970 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 ``` **Output of `podman info --debug`:** ``` host: arch: amd64 buildahVersion: 1.18.0 cgroupManager: systemd cgroupVersion: v1 conmon: package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon' path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.0.22, commit: ' cpus: 48 distribution: distribution: ubuntu version: "18.04" eventLogger: journald hostname: controller-1 idMappings: gidmap: null uidmap: null kernel: 4.15.0-128-generic linkmode: dynamic memFree: 126855479296 memTotal: 135093325824 ociRuntime: name: runc package: 'runc: /usr/sbin/runc' path: /usr/sbin/runc version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev' os: linux remoteSocket: path: /run/podman/podman.sock rootless: false slirp4netns: executable: "" package: "" version: "" swapFree: 137338286080 swapTotal: 137338286080 uptime: 20h 8m 0.15s (Approximately 0.83 days) registries: search: - docker.io - quay.io store: configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 2 paused: 0 running: 1 stopped: 1 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: {} graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: extfs Native Overlay Diff: "true" Supports d_type: "true" Using metacopy: "false" imageStore: number: 18 runRoot: /var/run/containers/storage volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 2.1.0 Built: 0 BuiltTime: Thu Jan 1 01:00:00 1970 GitCommit: "" GoVersion: go1.15.2 OsArch: linux/amd64 Version: 2.2.1 ``` **Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):** ``` podman/unknown,now 2.2.1~4 amd64 [installed] ``` **Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?** Yes => using last version and nothing about rootfull container **Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):** Physical server ubuntu 18.04.5

comments.body: Podman does NOT automatically open a Hole in your firewall rules.  So you need to open the firewall rules for port 80.
I do not need to open anything because chain INPUT uses an accept policy

Please provide ‘iptables -t nat -nvL’ and ‘iptables -nvL’ both with and
without the container in question running. Also , do you use any external
software (e.g. firewalld) to manage the system firewall?

On Wed, Dec 23, 2020 at 08:30 RomainNakedCat <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> I do not need to open anything because chain INPUT uses an accept policy
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/8814#issuecomment-750301475>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCDD5RYKLJ3AQGCGLELSWHWIJANCNFSM4VG7RJ2Q>
> .
>

Hello,

When I checked iptables rules i figured out the problem. I'm migrating from Docker to Podman but `apt purge docker.io` didnt remove any docker chains. Docker was taking over Podman that's why it didnt work. It's not a Podman issue.

Sorry for the inconvenience
